drills.name: Control and Shoot (Lower Ability)
drills.setup: 4m x 2m goal. Starting cone 11 metres out. Shooting cone 5–7 metres out.
instructions: One coach per four players
Coach rolls ball out slowly to player
Player must control ball then dribble and shoot within three seconds
Score one point per successful control/shot
Same player repeats three times then goes to back of line
With mobile players, roll ball slightly to one side to make player move to get behind ball
drills.when_to_use: Teaching positional play and teaching players to react quickly in game-like situations

drills.name: Control and Shoot with Chasers (Intermediate)
drills.setup: 4m x 2m goal. Shooting cone at 14 metres. Defender's cone at 18 metres.
instructions: Divide players into three teams: shooting team, defending team and goalkeeping team
Coach stands to side of goal and rolls ball out to shooter
Shooter controls ball, then dribbles and shoots
As shooter receives ball, defender is released to chase and tackle
Shooter can shoot at any time
Goalkeepers must remain within 1 metre of goal line
Players return to group after shot
Rotate groups after five minutes
drills.when_to_use: Good when preparing players for games, increases correct thinking under pressure

drills.name: Dribble in the Square (Lower Ability)
drills.setup: 14m x 14m square
instructions: Designate a 14m x 14m square
Every player has a football
Players dribble around inside of square, avoiding collisions
Encourage use of both feet
Encourage changing direction while looking up
Introduce different dribbling techniques
Coach can play as chaser — players try to avoid being kicked out
Play for 30 seconds
Award one point for keeping ball safe
drills.when_to_use: Fun exercise while still teaching techniques. Good for player awareness and confidence.

drills.name: Cone Drills
drills.setup: Seven cones in a straight line with a gate using cones at either side
instructions: Set up seven cones in a straight line
Place a gate using cones at either side
Demonstrate first without ball, then with ball
Have players weave through cones slowly without ball, then with ball
Correct players who lose control or weave too wide
Encourage players to use both feet
coaching_points: Awareness of body positioning when weaving
drills.when_to_use: Build players' confidence where emphasis is on praise. Begin walking then progress to passing drills.

drills.name: Immediate Attack — Three vs. Three with Dribbling Condition (Intermediate)
drills.setup: Minifield 18m x 14m with 4m-wide goals
instructions: Play on minifield with 4m-wide goals
Form two teams of three, one player is goalkeeper
Play regular game except: when player gets possession, must immediately dribble against an opponent
Cannot pass until they have attempted to beat an opponent
Award one point for successfully dribbling past opponent
Three points for each goal scored
coaching_points: Learn ability to think and react quickly under pressure
drills.when_to_use: Players who are beginning to express themselves on the field

drills.name: Passing Numbers
drills.setup: Players in pairs, 3 metres apart
instructions: Players in pairs and 3 metres apart
Player passes ball to partner
Players can be timed to see how many complete passes they make in one minute
Repeat
drills.when_to_use: Foundational passing drill

drills.name: Passing in Triangle
drills.setup: Players stand in triangle 3 metres apart
instructions: Players stand in triangle 3 metres apart
Pass ball to each other in clockwise direction
Pass ball in counterclockwise direction
Can also be done in a square using four players
drills.when_to_use: Increase passing skills from different angles

drills.name: Football Bowling (Lower Ability)
drills.setup: 10-metre-by-10-metre area with 5–10 cones up the middle
instructions: Designate 10-metre-by-10-metre area
Place 5–10 cones up the middle
Form teams of three or four players
Players pass and try to knock down cones
Coach retrieves and returns balls from middle
Keep time to see how long it takes to knock down all cones, or play one group against another
coaching_points: Stress accuracy of pass, not speed
Knocking over the cones is the goal
drills.when_to_use: Training sessions related to passing skills

drills.name: Player in the Middle Passing (Intermediate)
drills.setup: 15-metre-by-15-metre square with one player in middle
instructions: Designate 15-metre-by-15-metre square
Position one player in middle (initially the coach)
Three players stand on outside of square with ball
See how many passes three players make before being intercepted
Rotate middle player every two minutes
Show players how to move to open corner to get open for pass
See which group gets most passes
coaching_points: Keep ball moving at all times
Middle player must always be alert
drills.when_to_use: End of training sessions when players are warmed up to increase awareness in game-like situations

drills.name: Lower Ability Shooting — Distance
drills.setup: Cones at 1-, 2-, 3- and 4-metre intervals
instructions: Set up cones at 1-, 2-, 3- and 4-metre intervals
Line up and shoot ball as far as possible
Record distance
drills.when_to_use: Teaching correct technique and building confidence in shooting

drills.name: Lower Ability Shooting Accuracy
drills.setup: Two cones 2 metres apart at a distance of 5 metres
instructions: Set up two cones 2 metres apart at distance of 5 metres
Player shoots through cones (goals)
Five shots per player
Record goals accurately scored using correct technique
drills.when_to_use: Foundation shooting accuracy work

drills.name: Shoot Out! (Lower Ability)
drills.setup: Regular goal or marker cones. Starting cone 15–20 metres from goal.
instructions: Use regular goal or marker cones
Place starting cone 15–20 metres from goal
Three players on shooting team, three players on goalkeeping team
Shooter has three seconds to dribble and shoot
Coach counts down 3-2-1-shoot
Goalkeepers rotate after each shot
Five shots each, then change teams
coaching_points: Players position themselves correctly before using correct shooting technique
drills.when_to_use: Practicing shooting for goal

drills.name: One vs. One to Goal (Intermediate)
drills.setup: 20-metre-by-20-metre square with goals at opposite ends
instructions: Designate 20-metre-by-20-metre square with goals at opposite ends
Form two teams of three players
Number equally matched players 1-2-3
Play No. 1 from each team with No. 2 in goal and No. 3 behind goal as chaser
Play one vs. one for one minute
Coach rolls in spare balls when needed
One point for each shot on target, three points per goal
Rotate players
Keep team score
coaching_points: When player gets past defender must position correctly before shooting
drills.when_to_use: Any training session to perfect forward play or defense

drills.name: Tackle the Coach (Lower Ability)
drills.setup: 14m x 14m square. Each coach has a football.
instructions: Designate 14m x 14m square
Each coach has a football
Players try to pursue and tackle coaches
Coaches can call out name of specific players who try to tackle
One point for making any kind of foot contact with coach's ball
Higher ability: ball must be cleared out of square to score a point
coaching_points: Do not tackle unless you are sure you will win the ball
Players love to beat the coach
drills.when_to_use: Fun exercise for teaching commitment to tackle

drills.name: One vs. One vs. One (Intermediate)
drills.setup: 9m x 14m square with one ball per group of three
instructions: Designate 9m x 14m square
Form groups of three with one ball. One group plays while others rest
Two players stand at one end with ball, third player at other end
Player No.1 dribbles, tries to get past No.2 and over end line
Player No.2 tries to tackle and regain possession or force ball out
If No.2 wins ball, dribbles against No.3
If No.2 is beaten, No.1 turns around and dribbles against No.3
Award one point per successful tackle
drills.when_to_use: Assists timing of tackles and increases friendly competition

drills.name: Head to Coach (Lower Ability)
drills.setup: 14m x 14m square. One coach per three players.
instructions: Designate 14m x 14m square
Assign one coach per three players, each coach with a soccer ball
Players jog around coaches
Coach calls player's name then gently tosses ball to be headed back
One point for successful header
Introduce surprise commands like 'freeze', 'sit', 'all to the center'
drills.when_to_use: Use as part of individual skills. Good for technique correction.

drills.name: Head on Goal (Intermediate)
drills.setup: 4m x 2m goal. Marker cone 11 metres in front of goal.
instructions: Divide players into two teams: attacking team and goalkeeping team
Set up goal and marker cone 11 metres in front
Coach stands to side of goal and throws ball underhand to oncoming attacker
Attacker attempts to head ball and score
Goalkeepers rotate after each shot
Each player gets three attempts then switch teams
coaching_points: Let this be a fun exercise
drills.when_to_use: End of session to allow players to wind down with heading practice

drills.name: Goalkeeper Fundamentals (Lower Ability)
drills.setup: Triangle using large cones or flags, 4 metres per side. Coach in middle.
instructions: Set up triangle using large cones or flags, 4 metres per side
Position goalkeeper on each side of triangle
Coach stands in middle with several balls
Go around group working on basic goalkeeping fundamentals
Goalkeepers throw ball back to coach
Do same but throw randomly to keep goalkeepers alert
drills.when_to_use: Lower level players or beginning of training

drills.name: Goalie in the Middle (Intermediate)
drills.setup: Central goal 4m x 2m (or full-size for 11-a-side)
instructions: Mark out central goal with flags or cones
Goalkeeper stands in middle
Goalkeeper receives different kinds of shots (easy at first, build up)
Goalkeeper takes ball from one side then quickly turns to take shot from other side
Count number of saves made before a goal is scored
drills.when_to_use: Increasing agility and quick reactions

drills.name: Two vs. Two — Teammates
drills.setup: 18m x 9m field with goals at each end
instructions: Designate 18m x 9m field with 4m x 2m goals
Form four teams of two. Two teams stand behind each goal
Coach at halfway line with several balls
Teams come out to play two vs. two
If ball goes out of play, leave it and receive another from coach
Defending player must pass to teammate before trying to score
For lower ability players, allow each team to dribble and pass without opposition first
drills.when_to_use: Teaching team concepts and teammate identification

drills.name: One vs. One — Attack/Defend
drills.setup: 14m x 9m area with goals at each end
instructions: Designate 14m x 9m area with goals at each end
Form two teams of three wearing different colored bibs, number players 1-2-3
When a player's number is called, they come out and play for 30–45 seconds
Other players wait behind goal and collect balls
If ball goes out, coach rolls in another ball immediately
coaching_points: Which goal are you scoring in?
What do you do when you have the ball? (Attack)
What do you do when opponent has the ball? (Defend)
drills.when_to_use: Teaching basic attack and defend concepts

drills.name: Three vs. Three — Positions
drills.setup: 23m x 14m field with goals
instructions: Designate 23m x 14m field with 4m x 2m goals
Form two teams of three without goalkeepers (or 4v4 with goalkeeper)
Designate positions: right wing/left wing/center
Play regular three vs. three with kick-ins for ball over sidelines
Coach how to play in each part of field
Focus on wing players
Occasionally call freeze — players stop and check positions
Award points for good positional play
coaching_points: Great football players play as a ___? (Team)
drills.when_to_use: Teaching positions and team formation
